title: Noise-robust real-time extraction of the respiratory motion signal from pet list-data
abstract: A respiratory motion signal generation method operates on emission data (22) of an imaging subject in an imaging field of view (FOV) acquired by a positron emission tomography (PET) or single photon emission computed tomography (SPECT) imaging device (10). An array of regions (32) is defined in the imaging FOV without reference to anatomy of the imaging subject. For each region of the array of regions defined in the imaging FOV, an activity position versus time curve (54) is computed from the emission data acquired by the PET or SPECT imaging device. Frequency-selective filtering of the activity position versus time curves is performed to generate filtered activity position versus time curves. At least one motion signal (66) is generated by combining the filtered activity position versus time curves of at least a selected sub-set of the regions.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. An emission imaging data processing device comprising: an electronic processor (20); and a non-transitory storage medium storing instructions readable and executable by the electronic processor to perform a respiratory motion signal generation method including: operating a positron emission tomography (PET) or single photon emission computed tomography (SPECT) imaging device (10) to acquire emission data (22) of an imaging subject in an imaging field of view (FOV); for each region of an array of regions (32) defined in the imaging FOV, computing an activity position versus time curve (54) from the emission data acquired by the PET or SPECT imaging device; and generating at least one respiratory motion signal (66) by combining the activity position versus time curves of at least a sub-set of the regions of the array of regions after frequency- selective filtering of the activity position versus time curves to select content in a respiratory frequency band. 2. The emission imaging data processing device of claim 1 wherein the respiratory motion signal generation method further includes: selecting a sub-set of the regions of the array of regions (32) for which the activity position versus time curve (54) includes content in the respiratory frequency band satisfying a region selection criterion (62); wherein the at least one respiratory motion signal (66) is generated by combining the activity position versus time curves of only the selected sub-set of regions of the array of regions after frequency- selective filtering of the activity position versus time curves to select content in the respiratory frequency band. 3. The emission imaging data processing device of claim 2 wherein: the selection of the sub- set of the regions of the array of regions (32) is performed for initial successive time intervals; and 

 after the selection of the sub-set of regions of the array of regions, the computing of the activity position versus time curve (54) and the generating of the at least one respiratory motion signal (66) is performed using only the selected sub-set of regions of the array of regions for subsequent successive time intervals that follow the initial successive time intervals. 4. The emission imaging data processing device of any one of claims 2-3 wherein the respiratory motion signal generation method further includes: outputting a zero value for the respiratory motion signal (66) if the selected sub-set of regions of the array of regions includes less than a threshold number of regions satisfying the region selection criterion (62). 5. The emission imaging data processing device of any one of claims 1-4 wherein the computing of the activity position versus time curve (54) and the generating of the at least one respiratory motion signal (66) are performed concurrently with the operating of the PET or SPECT imaging device (10) to acquire emission data (22) of the imaging subject in the imaging FOV, and the respiratory motion signal generation method further includes: operating a display (26) to display the respiratory motion signal as a function of time during the operation of the PET or SPECT imaging device. 6. The emission imaging data processing device of any one of claims 1-5 wherein the respiratory motion signal generation method further includes: defining the array of regions (32) in the imaging FOV without reference to anatomy of the imaging subject. 7. The emission imaging data processing device of any one of claims 1-6 wherein the respiratory motion signal generation method further includes: defining the array of regions (32) in the imaging FOV with overlapping of the regions. 8. The emission imaging data processing device of any one of claims 1-7 wherein the computing of the activity position versus time curve (54) from the emission data (22) acquired by the PET or SPECT imaging device (10) includes: generating an activity map of the region for successive time intervals from the emission data acquired by the PET or SPECT imaging device; and 

 computing values of a statistical activity position descriptor for the activity maps of the region for the successive time intervals. 9. The emission imaging data processing device of claim 8 wherein the statistical activity position descriptor includes a position of the centroid of the activity map along a transverse direction parallel with an axial anatomical axis (z) of the imaging subject. 10. The emission imaging data processing device of any one of claims 8-9 wherein the statistical activity position descriptor includes a minimum distance of the centroid of the activity map from an axial anatomical axis (z) of the imaging subject. 11. The emission imaging data processing device of any one of claims 8-10 wherein: the operating comprises operating a time-of-flight (TOF) PET imaging device (10) to acquire emission data (22) comprising lines of response (LORs) with TOF localization along the LORs; and the generating of the activity map of the region for successive time intervals from the emission data comprises back-positioning each LOR to its maximum TOF likelihood position along the LOR. 12. The emission imaging data processing device of any one of claims 1-11 wherein the generating of the at least one respiratory motion signal (66) includes: frequency- selective filtering the activity position versus time curves (54) to select content in the respiratory frequency band; computing a correlation of each filtered activity position versus time curve with a reference respiratory motion signal; and adding or subtracting each filtered activity position versus time curve to the respiratory motion signal wherein the adding or subtracting is chosen based on a sign of the correlation. 13. The emission imaging data processing device of any one of claims 1-12 wherein the generating of the at least one respiratory motion signal (66) includes: frequency- selective filtering the activity position versus time curves (54) to select content in the respiratory frequency band; computing a correlation of each filtered activity position versus time curve with a reference respiratory motion signal; 

 dividing the imaging FOV into two or more portions based on the computed correlations; and for each portion of the imaging FOV, combining the filtered activity position versus time curves for regions in that portion of the imaging FOV to generate a respiratory motion signal for that portion of the imaging FOV. 14. A motion signal generation method operating on emission data (22) of an imaging subject in an imaging field of view (FOV) acquired by a positron emission tomography (PET) or single photon emission computed tomography (SPECT) imaging device (10), the motion signal generation method comprising: defining an array of regions (32) in the imaging FOV without reference to anatomy of the imaging subject; for each region of the array of regions defined in the imaging FOV, computing an activity position versus time curve (54) from the emission data acquired by the PET or SPECT imaging device; performing frequency- selective filtering of the activity position versus time curves to generate filtered activity position versus time curves; and generating at least one motion signal (66) by combining the filtered activity position versus time curves. 15. The motion signal generation method of claim 14 further comprising: selecting a sub-set of the regions of the array of regions (32) for which the activity position versus time curve (54) includes content in a frequency band satisfying a region selection criterion (62); wherein the at least one motion signal (66) is generated by combining the filtered activity position versus time curves of only the selected sub-set of regions of the array of regions. 16. The motion signal generation method of any one of claims 14-15 wherein the array of regions (32) in the imaging FOV is defined with overlapping of the regions. 17. The motion signal generation method of any one of claims 14-16 wherein the computing of the activity position versus time curve (54) from the emission data (22) acquired by the PET or SPECT imaging device includes: 

 generating an activity map of the region for successive time intervals from the emission data acquired by the PET or SPECT imaging device; and computing values of a statistical activity position descriptor for the activity maps of the region for the successive time intervals. 18. The motion signal generation method of claim 17 wherein the emission data (22) of the imaging subject in the imaging FOV is acquired by a PET imaging device (10) with TOF localization, and the generating of the activity map of the region for successive time intervals from the emission data comprises back-positioning each line of response (LOR) of the emission data to its maximum TOF likelihood position along the LOR. 19. The motion signal generation method of any one of claims 14-18 wherein the generating of the at least one motion signal (66) includes: computing a correlation of each filtered activity position versus time curve (54) with a reference motion signal; and adding or subtracting each filtered position versus time curve to the respiratory motion signal wherein the adding or subtracting is chosen based on a sign of the correlation. 20. The motion signal generation method of any one of claims 14-19 wherein: performing frequency- selective filtering of the activity position versus time curves to generate filtered activity position versus time curves (54) comprises bandpass filtering the activity position versus time curves using a bandpass filter having a respiratory frequency passband; and the generating comprises generating the at least one motion signal (66) as a respiratory motion signal by combining the bandpass filtered activity position versus time curves. 21. An emission imaging data processing device comprising: a positron emission tomography (PET) or single photon emission computed tomography (SPECT) imaging device (10); an electronic processor (20); and a non-transitory storage medium storing instructions readable and executable by the electronic processor to perform a respiratory motion signal generation method including: operating the PET or SPECT imaging device to acquire emission data 

 computing activity maps from the emission data for successive time intervals in a region defined in the imaging FOV; computing a transaxial activity position versus time curve from the activity maps, the transaxial activity position comprising a minimum distance of the centroid of the activity map from an axial anatomical axis (z) of the imaging subject; and generating a respiratory motion signal (66) based on at least the transaxial activity position versus time curve. 22. The emission imaging data processing device of claim 21, wherein the respiratory motion signal generation method further includes: computing a transverse activity position versus time curve from the activity maps, the transverse activity position comprising a position of the centroid of the activity map along a transverse direction parallel with an axial anatomical axis (z) of the imaging subject; wherein the respiratory motion signal is generated further based on the transverse activity position versus time curve.
